items.headline: Thunder Sweep Suns, Advance to Western Conference Semifinals!
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder completed a dominant 4-0 sweep of the Phoenix Suns last night, punching their ticket to the Western Conference Semifinals. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander led the charge with another stellar performance.

items.headline: Magic One Win Away from Upsetting Top-Seeded Pistons!
items.summary: The Orlando Magic are on the verge of a major upset, taking a commanding 3-1 series lead over the #1 seeded Detroit Pistons after a gritty Game 4 victory. They now look to close out the series in Game 5.

items.headline: Rockets Force Game 5
items.summary: Houston avoids elimination with a strong defensive effort.

items.headline: Thunder Complete Sweep
items.summary: OKC dominates to advance past the Suns.

items.headline: Magic Take 3-1 Lead
items.summary: Orlando pushes top seed Detroit to the brink.

items.headline: Spurs Dominate Second Half to Take 3-1 Lead
items.summary: Victor Wembanyama leads San Antonio over Portland in Game 4.

items.headline: Nuggets Avoid Elimination
items.summary: Denver keeps their playoff hopes alive against the Timberwolves.

items.headline: Damian Lillard Out for Season with Achilles Injury
items.summary: The Portland Trail Blazers will be without star guard Damian Lillard for the remainder of the season due to an Achilles injury, a significant blow to their playoff aspirations.

items.headline: Joel Embiid Probable for Game 5 Despite Abdominal Issue
items.summary: Philadelphia 76ers star Joel Embiid is listed as probable for tonight's crucial Game 5 against the Boston Celtics, despite battling an abdominal issue. His presence will be vital for the Sixers.

items.headline: Spurs' David Jones Garcia Out for Season
items.summary: San Antonio Spurs forward David Jones Garcia has undergone ankle surgery and will miss the rest of the 2025-26 season.

items.headline: Tonight's Playoff Schedule (April 28)
items.summary: Get ready for a thrilling night of playoff basketball! The Philadelphia 76ers face the Boston Celtics at 7:00 PM ET, followed by the Atlanta Hawks against the New York Knicks at 8:00 PM ET. Later, the Portland Trail Blazers visit the San Antonio Spurs at 9:30 PM ET.
